exercises.title: Sleep Quality Audit
exercises.description: Track your sleep for 1 week using a simple log: bedtime, wake time, sleep quality (1-10), and factors that affected sleep (caffeine, screens, stress). Then implement 3 sleep hygiene changes and track for another week. Compare the results.
hints: Use a sleep tracker app or simple notebook by your bed
Be honest about factors — did you check your phone at 1 AM?
Implement changes gradually (don't overhaul everything at once)

solution.code:
SLEEP TRACKING LOG:

WEEK 1 (BEFORE CHANGES):
| Day | Bedtime | Wake  | Quality | Factors               |
|-----|---------|-------|---------|-----------------------|
| Mon | 11:30 PM| 7:00  | 5/10    | Phone in bed, coffee 3 PM |
| Tue | 12:00 AM| 7:00  | 4/10    | Late coding, anxiety   |
| Wed | 11:00 PM| 6:45  | 6/10    | Better, still phone    |
| Thu | 11:45 PM| 7:00  | 5/10    | Coffee 4 PM, screens   |
| Fri | 12:15 AM| 7:30  | 4/10    | Late night, alcohol    |
| Sat | 1:00 AM | 9:00  | 5/10    | Weekend, inconsistent  |
| Sun | 10:30 PM| 6:30  | 7/10    | Early bed, better      |

Average: 5.1/10

CHANGES IMPLEMENTED:
1. No caffeine after 2 PM
2. Phone in drawer after 9:30 PM
3. Consistent 10:30 PM bedtime

WEEK 2 (AFTER CHANGES):
| Day | Bedtime | Wake  | Quality | Factors               |
|-----|---------|-------|---------|-----------------------|
| Mon | 10:30 PM| 6:30  | 7/10    | Followed routine       |
| Tue | 10:30 PM| 6:30  | 8/10    | Felt great             |
| Wed | 10:45 PM| 6:30  | 7/10    | Slight deviation       |
| Thu | 10:30 PM| 6:30  | 8/10    | Deep sleep noted       |
| Fri | 10:30 PM| 6:30  | 8/10    | Best night yet         |
| Sat | 11:00 PM| 7:00  | 7/10    | Weekend, slightly late |
| Sun | 10:30 PM| 6:30  | 8/10    | Ready for week         |

Average: 7.6/10 (+49% improvement)

KEY FINDINGS:
• Caffeine cutoff at 2 PM was the biggest single improvement
• Phone in drawer removed the 'one last check' habit
• Consistent bedtime regulated circadian rhythm
• Sleep debt from Week 1 took ~3 days to recover

exercises.title: Workspace Ergonomics Setup
exercises.description: Audit your current workspace against the ergonomics guide. Take a photo, identify the top 3 issues, and fix them. Common fixes: raise monitor to eye level (use books if needed), adjust chair height, position keyboard so elbows are at 90 degrees. Set an hourly reminder to check your posture.
hints: Take a photo from the side — it reveals posture issues you don't feel
The monitor height is the most common issue — top of screen should be at eye level
Use the 20-20-20 rule for eye strain: every 20 min, look 20 feet away for 20 sec

solution.code:
ERGONOMICS AUDIT RESULTS:

BEFORE FIXES (from photo analysis):
❌ Monitor too low (looking down — neck strain)
❌ Chair too high (feet dangling — back pain)
❌ Keyboard too far forward (reaching — shoulder tension)
✓ Mouse position: OK
✓ Room lighting: OK

FIXES IMPLEMENTED:
1. Monitor: Raised 4 inches using monitor stand ($25)
   → Eye level now, neck angle improved
2. Chair: Lowered seat, added footrest ($15)
   → Feet flat, back supported
3. Keyboard: Pulled closer, elbows at 90 degrees
   → Shoulders relaxed, wrists straight

HOURLY POSTURE REMINDER (phone alarm):
□ Shoulders relaxed? (drop them away from ears)
□ Back against chair? (don't lean forward)
□ Feet flat on floor? (or footrest)
□ Eyes level with screen? (not looking up/down)
□ Wrists straight on keyboard? (not bent)

RESULTS (after 1 week):
• Neck pain: Reduced from daily to 0/week
• Shoulder tension: Reduced significantly
• Eye strain: Improved (20-20-20 rule helped)
• Lower back pain: Reduced from 3/week to 1/week
• Overall comfort: Much improved

exercises.title: Build a Morning Movement Routine
exercises.description: Design a 20-minute morning exercise routine that fits before work. Include: 5 minutes of dynamic stretching, 10 minutes of moderate exercise (walking, jogging, yoga), and 5 minutes of cooldown. Track completion and energy levels for 2 weeks.
hints: Morning exercise is ideal — it improves focus during peak work hours
Start with walking if you're not exercising regularly — it's sustainable
Prep workout clothes the night before (reduce activation energy)

solution.code:
MORNING MOVEMENT ROUTINE:

PREP (night before):
• Lay out workout clothes by bed
• Set alarm 20 min earlier than usual
• Fill water bottle

ROUTINE (20 minutes):
5 MIN - DYNAMIC STRETCHING:
• Neck rolls (30 sec each direction)
• Arm circles (30 sec forward, 30 sec backward)
• Hip circles (30 sec each direction)
• Leg swings (30 sec each leg)
• Cat-cow stretches (1 min)

10 MIN - MODERATE EXERCISE (choose one):
• Option A: Brisk walk around neighborhood
• Option B: Yoga flow (sun salutations)
• Option C: Bodyweight circuit (push-ups, squats, planks)
• Option D: Jump rope or dancing

5 MIN - COOLDOWN:
• Deep breathing (1 min)
• Hamstring stretch (1 min)
• Shoulder stretch (1 min)
• Hip flexor stretch (1 min)
• Final deep breath (1 min)

TRACKING (2 weeks):
| Week | Completed | Energy at 10 AM | Focus (1-10) | Notes        |
|------|-----------|-----------------|-------------|-------------|
| 1    | 4/5 days  | 7/10            | 7/10        | Hard to wake |
| 1    | 3/5 days  | 6/10            | 6/10        | Missed 2 days|
| 2    | 5/5 days  | 8/10            | 8/10        | Getting easier|
| 2    | 4/5 days  | 7/10            | 8/10        | Consistent   |

RESULTS:
• Average completion: 80% (target: 80%+)
• Energy improvement: +1.5 points at 10 AM
• Focus improvement: +1.5 points
• Sleep quality: Improved (exhaustion helps)
• Conclusion: Morning exercise is worth the 20 minutes
